heading: Bowling Alley Point vs Bowman — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bowling Alley Point, New South Wales and Bowman, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bowling Alley Point has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Bowman (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bowling Alley Point or Bowman safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bowling Alley Point has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Bowman scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bowling Alley Point scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Bowman scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bowling Alley Point or Bowman?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bowling Alley Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
